title: Chapter 5 Quiz: Loops
description: Test your knowledge of while, for, foreach, break, continue, and more!

questions.question: A do-while loop always executes at least once.
questions.explanation: The condition is checked after the body, so it runs at least once.
Body runs once ($i becomes 1), then condition $i < 0 is false, so stops.

questions.question: In a for loop, all three expressions (init, condition, increment) are required.
questions.explanation: Any can be omitted, but the semicolons must remain.
Prints 0,1; when i=2, break stops loop.

questions.question: The keyword to skip the rest of the current loop iteration is ______.
Using `break 2` inside a nested loop will exit only the inner loop.
questions.explanation: `break 2` exits two levels of loops (inner and outer).

questions.question: The foreach loop can only iterate over indexed arrays.
questions.explanation: foreach works on both indexed and associative arrays, as well as objects.

questions.question: The `continue` statement can be used inside a switch.
questions.explanation: `continue` in switch behaves like `break` unless used inside loops. In loops, continue works as expected.

When i=1,j=1, continue 2 skips to next i=2, so [1,2] never prints. Let's compute: i=0: all j; i=1: j=0 prints [1,0]; j=1 triggers continue 2, so no j=2; i=2: all j print. So output includes [1,0] and all i=2 pairs.

questions.question: In a foreach loop, to modify the original array, you use the ______ operator.
An infinite loop can be created with for(;;).
questions.explanation: Yes, for(;;) with no condition is an infinite loop (requires break to exit).
